race.name: John Craig Memorial
race.tagline: A memorial citizen ski race in the Pacific Northwest mountains — honoring a local skiing pioneer with high-altitude Nordic racing.

climate.description: Heavy Pacific Northwest snowfall at Snoqualmie Pass. Temperatures typically -5°C to 2°C. Wet, dense snow is common.
challenges: Variable conditions — rain possible at lower elevations
Mountain pass weather can change rapidly

course.primary: Mountain trail course near Snoqualmie Pass in the Cascades. Rolling terrain through heavy Pacific Northwest snowpack.
features: Memorial event honoring local skiing pioneer

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: A small memorial race with Cascade mountain setting but very limited field and organization.

history.summary: The John Craig Memorial is a small citizen ski race in Washington's Cascade Mountains, held in memory of a local Nordic skiing pioneer.
notable_facts: Memorial event honoring a Pacific Northwest skiing pioneer
Set in the Cascade Range near Snoqualmie Pass
Part of the Pacific Northwest Nordic community
